In devela::sys::env

devela::sys::env

Struct Env 

Source 
pub struct Env;
Expand description

🛠️ A namespaced wrapper for std::env functions and constants.

📍 sys/os

Implementations§

Source§

impl Env

§Constants

Source

pub const TARGET: &str = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

The full Rust target triple.

Mirrors Cargo’s TARGET build-script environment variable.

Source

pub const ARCH: &str = "x86_64"

The Rust target_arch value for the current compilation target.

Mirrors CARGO_CFG_TARGET_ARCH.

Example values include "x86_64", "aarch64", "riscv64", and "wasm32".

See also std::env::consts::ARCH.

Source

pub const OS: &str = "linux"

The specific operating system in use.

Mirrors CARGO_CFG_TARGET_OS.

Example values include "linux", "windows", "macos", and "none".

See also std::env::consts::OS.

Source

pub const VENDOR: &str = "unknown"

The target vendor.

Mirrors CARGO_CFG_TARGET_VENDOR.

Common values include "pc", "apple", and "unknown".

Source

pub const FAMILY: &str = "unix"

The primary target family.

Usually "unix", "windows", "wasm", "itron", or "".

For all target families, see Self::FAMILIES.

See also std::env::consts::FAMILY.

Source

pub const FAMILIES: &str = "unix"

All target families, comma-separated.

Mirrors CARGO_CFG_TARGET_FAMILY.

This can contain more than one value.

Source

pub const ENV: &str = "gnu"

The target environment/disambiguator.

Mirrors CARGO_CFG_TARGET_ENV.

Usually "", "gnu", "msvc", or "musl".

Source

pub const ABI: &str = ""

The target ABI/disambiguator.

Mirrors CARGO_CFG_TARGET_ABI.

Usually ""; examples include "eabihf", "llvm", and "abi64".

Source

pub const ENDIAN: &str = "little"

The target endianness.

Usually "little" or "big".

Source

pub const POINTER_WIDTH: &str = "64"

The target pointer width, in bits.

Usually "16", "32", or "64".

Source

pub const DLL_PREFIX: &str = "lib"

The filename prefix used for shared libraries on this platform.

Usually "lib" or "".

See also std::env::consts::DLL_PREFIX.

Source

pub const DLL_EXTENSION: &str = "so"

The file extension used for shared libraries on this platform, without the dot.

Usually "so", "dylib", "dll", or "".

See also std::env::consts::DLL_EXTENSION.

Source

pub const DLL_SUFFIX: &str = ".so"

The filename suffix used for shared libraries on this platform.

Usually ".so", ".dylib", ".dll", or "".

See also std::env::consts::DLL_SUFFIX.

Source

pub const EXE_EXTENSION: &str = ""

The file extension used for executable binaries on this platform, without the dot.

Usually "exe" or "".

Other target-specific values include "bin", "elf", "js", and "wasm".

See also std::env::consts::EXE_EXTENSION.

Source

pub const EXE_SUFFIX: &str = ""

The filename suffix used for executable binaries on this platform.

Usually ".exe" or "".

This matches Self::EXE_EXTENSION with a leading dot when non-empty.

See also std::env::consts::EXE_SUFFIX.

Source§

impl Env

§Command line arguments

Source

pub fn args() -> Args ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns the process arguments as Strings.

Return type: std::env::Args.

See also std::env::args.

Source

pub fn args_os() -> ArgsOs ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns the process arguments as OsStrings.

Return type: std::env::ArgsOs.

See also std::env::args_os.

Source

pub fn args_os_ref() -> IterArgsOsRef ⓘ

Available on crate features std and unsafe_ffi only.

⚠️ Command line arguments by reference: Iterator<Item = &'static OsStr>.

§Vendored

This is adapted work from argv.

Source§

impl Env

§Environment variables

Source

pub fn var<K: AsRef<OsStr>>(key: K) -> Result<String, VarError> ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Fetches the environment variable key from the current process.

See also std::env::var.

Source

pub fn vars() -> Vars ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns an iterator of (variable, value) pairs of strings, for all the environment variables of the current process.

Return type: std::env::Vars.

See also: std::env::vars.

Source

pub fn var_os<K: AsRef<OsStr>>(key: K) -> Option<OsString> ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Fetches the environment variable key from the current process.

See std::env::var_os.

Source

pub fn vars_os() -> VarsOs ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns an iterator of (variable, value) pairs of OS strings, for all the environment variables of the current process.

Return type: std::env::VarsOs.

See std::env::vars_os.

Source

pub unsafe fn remove_var<K: AsRef<OsStr>>(key: K)

Available on crate features std and unsafe_thread only.

Removes the environment variable key from the environment of the currently running process.

§Safety

See std::env::remove_var.

Source

pub unsafe fn set_var<K: AsRef<OsStr>, V: AsRef<OsStr>>(key: K, value: V)

Available on crate features std and unsafe_thread only.

Sets the environment variable key to the value value for the currently running process.

§Safety

See std::env::set_var.

Source§

impl Env

§Paths

Source

pub fn current_exe() -> IoResult<PathBuf>

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns the full filesystem path of the current running executable.

See std::env::current_exe.

Source

pub fn current_dir() -> IoResult<PathBuf>

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns the current working directory.

See std::env::current_dir.

Source

pub fn set_current_dir<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> IoResult<()>

Available on crate feature std only.

Changes the current working directory to the specified path.

See std::env::set_current_dir.

Source

pub fn home_dir() -> Option<PathBuf> ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns the path of the current user’s home directory if known.

See std::env::home_dir.

Source

pub fn temp_dir() -> PathBuf ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Returns the path of a temporary directory.

See std::env::temp_dir.

Source

pub fn join_paths<I, T>(paths: I) -> Result<OsString, JoinPathsError> ⓘ
where I: IntoIterator<Item = T>, T: AsRef<OsStr>,

Available on crate feature std only.

Joins a collection of Paths appropriately for the PATH environment variable.

See std::env::join_paths.

Source

pub fn split_paths<T: AsRef<OsStr> + ?Sized>(unparsed: &T) -> SplitPaths<'_> ⓘ

Available on crate feature std only.

Parses input according to platform conventions for the PATH environment variable.

See std::env::split_paths.

Source§

fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> Result

Formats the value using the given formatter. Read more

where T: Any + ?Sized,

Source§

fn type_id() -> TypeId

Returns the TypeId of self. Read more

Returns the type name of self. Read more

fn type_is<T: 'static>(&self) -> bool

Returns true if Self is of type T. Read more
Source§

fn type_hash(&self) -> u64

Returns a deterministic hash of the TypeId of Self.
Source§

fn type_hash_with<H: Hasher>(&self, hasher: H) -> u64

Returns a deterministic hash of the TypeId of Self using a custom hasher.

where Self: Sized,

where Self: Sized,

where Self: Sized,

Available on crate feature alloc only.
Upcasts Box<self> as Box<dyn Any>. Read more

fn downcast_ref<T: 'static>(&self) -> Option<&T> ⓘ

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout and non-crate feature safe_code only.
Returns some shared reference to the inner value if it is of type T. Read more
Source§

fn downcast_mut<T: 'static>(&mut self) -> Option<&mut T> ⓘ

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout and non-crate feature safe_code only.
Returns some exclusive reference to the inner value if it is of type T. Read more

The size of this type in bytes.

Returns the size of this type in bytes. Read more

fn from(t: T) -> T

Returns the argument unchanged.

impl<T> Hook for T

Source§

fn hook<F>(self, f: F) -> Self
where F: FnOnce(&mut Self),

Hooks a mutation step into the value and returns it. Read more
Source§

fn tap<F>(self, f: F) -> Self
where F: FnOnce(&Self),

Taps into the value for observation and returns it unchanged. Read more

impl<T, U> Into<U> for T
where U: From<T>,

Source§

fn into(self) -> U

Calls U::from(self).

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of From<T> for U chooses to do.

Source§

where T: ?Sized,

Source§

const NEEDS_DROP: bool = _

Know whether dropping values of this type matters, in compile-time.

Returns the minimum alignment of the type in bytes. Read more

Returns the alignment of the pointed-to value in bytes. Read more
Source§

Returns the size of a type in bytes. Read more
Source§

fn mem_size_of_val(&self) -> usize

Returns the size of the pointed-to value in bytes. Read more

fn mem_copy(&self) -> Self
where Self: Copy,

Bitwise-copies a value. Read more
Source§

fn mem_needs_drop(&self) -> bool

Returns true if dropping values of this type matters. Read more
Source§

fn mem_drop(self)
where Self: Sized,

Drops self by running its destructor. Read more
Source§

fn mem_forget(self)
where Self: Sized,

Forgets about self without running its destructor. Read more
Source§

fn mem_replace(&mut self, other: Self) -> Self
where Self: Sized,

Replaces self with other, returning the previous value of self. Read more
Source§

fn mem_take(&mut self) -> Self
where Self: Default,

Replaces self with its default value, returning the previous value of self. Read more
Source§

fn mem_swap(&mut self, other: &mut Self)
where Self: Sized,

Swaps the value of self and other without deinitializing either one. Read more
Source§

unsafe fn mem_zeroed<T>() -> T

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout only.
Returns the value of type T represented by the all-zero byte-pattern. Read more
Source§

unsafe fn mem_transmute_copy<Src, Dst>(src: &Src) -> Dst

Available on crate feature unsafe_layout only.
Returns the value of type T represented by the all-zero byte-pattern. Read more

where Self: Sync + Unpin,

Available on crate feature unsafe_slice only.

where Self: Sync + Unpin,

where T: ?Sized,

Source§

fn morph<F>(self, f: F) -> R
where F: FnOnce(Self) -> R, Self: Sized,

Morphs the value into a new one and returns it. Read more
Source§

fn morph_ref<F>(&self, f: F) -> R
where F: FnOnce(&Self) -> R,

Morphs the value by shared reference and returns the result. Read more
Source§

fn morph_mut<F>(&mut self, f: F) -> R
where F: FnOnce(&mut Self) -> R,

Morphs the value by exclusive reference and returns the result. Read more

impl<T, U> TryFrom<U> for T
where U: Into<T>,

Source§

type Error = Infallible

The type returned in the event of a conversion error.
Source§

fn try_from(value: U) -> Result<T, <T as TryFrom<U>>::Error> ⓘ

Performs the conversion.
Source§

impl<T, U> TryInto<U> for T
where U: TryFrom<T>,

Source§

type Error = <U as TryFrom<T>>::Error

The type returned in the event of a conversion error.
Source§

fn try_into(self) -> Result<U, <U as TryFrom<T>>::Error> ⓘ

Performs the conversion.
